channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Black Hat USA
item.description: No summary available
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Geopolitics, AI, and Cybersecurity: Insights From RSAC 2026
item.description: AI-driven threats, global leadership shifts, and the future of cybersecurity in a rapidly evolving landscape were among the discussions at RSAC 2026 Conference....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Claude Code leak used to push infostealer malware on GitHub
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting the recent Claude Code source code leak by using fake GitHub repositories to deliver Vidar information-stealing malware. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Not Toying Around: Hasbro Attack May Take 'Weeks' to Remediate
item.description: The company's 8-K filing notes "unauthorized access" and that it's activated business continuity plans and taken some systems offline....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Hackers Exploit CVE-2025-55182 to Breach 766 Next.js Hosts, Steal Credentials
item.description: A large-scale credential harvesting operation has been observed exploiting the React2Shell vulnerability as an initial infection vector to steal database credentials, SSH private keys, Amazon Web Serv...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Security Bosses Are All-In on AI. Here's Why
item.description: CISOs are bullish on AI and have big plans to roll out future tools. We talk to Reddit CISO Frederick Lee and leading analyst Dave Gruber about how AI is working out in the real world, as well as its ...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Drift loses $280 million as hackers seize Security Council powers
item.description: The Drift Protocol lost at least $280 million after a threat actor took control of its Security Council administrative powers in a planned, sophisticated operation. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: RSAC 2026: AI Dominates, But Community Remains Key to Security
item.description: As AI took center stage at this year's conference, experts debated automation, oversight and the evolving role of human intelligence in cybersecurity — despite the US government's notable absence....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Residential proxies evaded IP reputation checks in 78% of 4B sessions
item.description: Researchers warn that residential proxies used to route malicious traffic are a big problem for IP reputation systems, as there is no clear distinction between attackers and legitimate users. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cisco Patches 9.8 CVSS IMC and SSM Flaws Allowing Remote System Compromise
item.description: Cisco has released updates to address a critical security flaw in the Integrated Management Controller (IMC) that, if successfully exploited, could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to bypass ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Adversaries Exploit Vacant Homes to Intercept Mail in Hybrid Cybercrime
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting vacant homes as "drop addresses" to intercept mail and enable fraud. Flare shows how postal services and fake identities are abused to turn mail into a fraud vector. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New Progress ShareFile flaws can be chained in pre-auth RCE attacks
item.description: Two vulnerabilities in Progress ShareFile, an enterprise-grade secure file transfer solution, can be chained to enable unauthenticated file exfiltration from affected environments. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Medtech giant Stryker fully operational after data-wiping attack
item.description: Stryker Corporation, one of the world's leading medical technology companies, says it's fully operational three weeks after many of its systems were wiped out in a cyberattack claimed by the Iranian-l...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Bank Trojan 'Casbaneiro' Worms Through Latin America
item.description: Augmented Marauder's multipronged banking-Trojan cyber campaigns are targeting Spanish speakers, evading detection, and replicating rapidly....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Pre-Auth Chains, Android Rootkits, CloudTrail Evasion & 10 More Stories
item.description: The latest ThreatsDay Bulletin is basically a cheat sheet for everything breaking on the internet right now. No corporate fluff or boring lectures here, just a quick and honest look at the messy reali...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Researchers Uncover Mining Operation Using ISO Lures to Spread RATs and Crypto Miners
item.description: A financially motivated operation codenamed REF1695 has been observed leveraging fake installers to deploy remote access trojans (RATs) and cryptocurrency miners since November 2023. "Beyond cryptomin...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The State of Trusted Open Source Report
item.description: In December 2025, we shared the first-ever The State of Trusted Open Source report, featuring insights from our product data and customer base on open source consumption across our catalog of containe...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Critical Cisco IMC auth bypass gives attackers Admin access
item.description: Cisco has patched several critical and high-severity vulnerabilities, including an Integrated Management Controller (IMC) authentication bypass that enables attackers to gain Admin access. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: WhatsApp Alerts 200 Users After Fake iOS App Installed Spyware; Italian Firm Faces Action
item.description: Meta-owned messaging platform WhatsApp said it alerted about 200 users who were tricked into installing a bogus version of its iOS app that was infected with spyware. According to reports from Italian...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft links Classic Outlook issue to email delivery problems
item.description: Microsoft is investigating a known issue that prevents some Classic Outlook users from sending emails via Outlook.com. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Over 14,000 F5 BIG-IP APM instances still exposed to RCE attacks
item.description: Internet security watchdog Shadowserver has found over 14,000 BIG-IP APM instances exposed online amid ongoing attacks exploiting a critical-severity remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Apple Expands iOS 18.7.7 Update to More Devices to Block DarkSword Exploit
item.description: Apple on Wednesday expanded the availability of iOS 18.7.7 and iPadOS 18.7.7 to a broader range of devices to protect users from the risk posed by a recently disclosed exploit kit known as DarkSword. ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New CrystalRAT malware adds RAT, stealer and prankware features
item.description: A new malware-as-a-service called CrystalRAT is being promoted on Telegram, offering remote access, data theft, keylogging, and clipboard hijacking capabilities. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Ransomware Will Hit Hospitals. Rehearsals Are Key to Defense
item.description: A chief medical information officer describes what hospitals face when they inevitably suffer a ransomware attack—whether it leads to short- or long-term outages....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Apple expands iOS 18 updates to more iPhones to block DarkSword attacks
item.description: Apple has now made it possible for more iPhones still running iOS 18 to receive security updates that protect against the actively exploited DarkSword exploit kit. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers exploit TrueConf zero-day to push malicious software updates
item.description: Hackers have targeted TrueConf conference servers in attacks that exploit a zero-day vulnerability, allowing them to execute arbitrary files on all connected endpoints. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New EvilTokens service fuels Microsoft device code phishing attacks
item.description: A new malicious kit called EvilTokens integrates device code phishing capabilities, allowing attackers to hijack Microsoft accounts and provide advanced features for business email compromise attacks....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: LatAm's Self-Taught Cyber Talent Overlooked Amid Cyberattack Glut
item.description: A newly released study exclusively shared with Dark Reading details the unique circumstances that make up Latin America's labor pool, and why organizations may want to expand their talent search....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: 'NoVoice' Android malware on Google Play infected 2.3 million devices
item.description: A new Android malware dubbed NoVoice exploited known vulnerabilities to gain root access and has been distributed through more than 50 apps on Google Play Store, with at least 2.3 million downloads. [...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cyberattacks Intensify Pressure on Latin American Governments
item.description: Cyber threats across Latin America are increasingly targeting government systems, from disruptive attacks in Puerto Rico to a surge of probes against Colombia’s health sector....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading
